name: WooCommerce
description: Open-source eCommerce plugin for WordPress providing REST APIs for store and admin (products, orders, customers, etc.).

auth_methods.note: Docs describe consumer key & consumer secret (API keys), HTTP Basic Auth (using consumer key as username and secret as password), and OAuth 1.0a for legacy integrations. JWT/bearer tokens are not listed as built-in methods.

credential_access.note: API keys (consumer key & secret) are generated in the WordPress admin (WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API) by site users—no vendor approval required.

api_surface.note: Official REST API v3 covers most store objects (products, orders, customers, coupons, settings, etc.). There is also a separate Store API for storefront/customer interactions (see store API docs).

buildability.note: Public REST docs and self-serve API key creation exist; a developer with access to a WooCommerce site can obtain credentials and use the REST or Store APIs immediately.

queries: WooCommerce REST API authentication consumer key consumer secret generate API keys WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API
WooCommerce REST API requires paid plan
